title: Auto-Scaling Trigger Algorithm
description: Algorithm for triggering auto-scaling based on CPU utilization thresholds
code:
ALGORITHM AutoScale
BEGIN
 SET current_cpu = GetCPUUtilization()
 SET current_users = GetConcurrentUsers()
 
 // Scale out conditions
 IF current_cpu > 80% AND scale_out_cooldown_expired THEN
 SET new_instances = current_instances + 1
 LaunchInstance(new_instances)
 SET last_scale_out_time = NOW()
 LOG "Scaled out to " + new_instances + " instances"
 END IF
 
 // Scale in conditions
 IF current_cpu < 30% AND current_instances > min_instances THEN
 SET new_instances = current_instances - 1
 TerminateInstance(new_instances)
 SET last_scale_in_time = NOW()
 LOG "Scaled in to " + new_instances + " instances"
 END IF
 
 // Emergency scale for viral traffic
 IF current_users > threshold_viral THEN
 SET new_instances = current_instances + 3
 LaunchInstance(new_instances)
 ALERT operations_team
 END IF
END
explanation: This algorithm monitors CPU utilization and user concurrency to automatically scale infrastructure. It includes scale-out for high load, scale-in for low load, and emergency scaling for viral traffic events.

title: API Rate Limiter
description: Token bucket algorithm implementation for API rate limiting
code:
ALGORITHM RateLimit(client_id, endpoint)
BEGIN
 SET bucket = GetBucket(client_id, endpoint)
 SET current_time = NOW()
 
 // Refill tokens based on time elapsed
 SET time_passed = current_time - bucket.last_refill
 SET tokens_to_add = time_passed * refill_rate
 SET bucket.tokens = MIN(max_tokens, bucket.tokens + tokens_to_add)
 SET bucket.last_refill = current_time
 
 // Check if request allowed
 IF bucket.tokens >= 1 THEN
 bucket.tokens = bucket.tokens - 1
 SaveBucket(client_id, endpoint, bucket)
 RETURN ALLOW(remaining_tokens = bucket.tokens)
 ELSE
 SET retry_after = (1 - bucket.tokens) / refill_rate
 RETURN DENY(retry_after_seconds = retry_after)
 END IF
END
explanation: Token bucket algorithm allows burst traffic while maintaining average rate. Tokens refill over time and requests consume tokens. If insufficient tokens, request is denied with retry time.

title: Cache Invalidation Strategy
description: TTL-based cache invalidation with event-driven updates for UGC
code:
ALGORITHM InvalidateCache(content_id, content_type)
BEGIN
 // Time-based expiration
 SET ttl = GetTTL(content_type)
 // Profile: 5 min, Post: 2 min, Comment: 1 min
 
 // Immediate invalidation on update
 PROCEDURE OnContentUpdate(content_id)
 DELETE cache_key("content:" + content_id)
 DELETE cache_key("user_feed:" + GetOwner(content_id))
 // Broadcast to all cache nodes
 PUBLISH("cache_invalidate", content_id)
 END PROCEDURE
 
 // Cascade invalidation for related content
 PROCEDURE OnUserUpdate(user_id)
 DELETE cache_key("user_profile:" + user_id)
 DELETE cache_key("user_stats:" + user_id)
 // Invalidate all affected user feeds
 followers = GetFollowers(user_id)
 FOR EACH follower IN followers
 DELETE cache_key("feed:" + follower)
 END FOR
 END PROCEDURE
END
explanation: This strategy combines TTL-based expiration with event-driven immediate invalidation. Different content types have different TTLs. Updates trigger cascade invalidation across related cached items.

complexity.time: O(n) where n = number of followers
